channel.title: Learning Mechanics
channel.description: The mathematical science of neural network training

item.title: Deep linear networks are a surprisingly useful toy model of weight-space dynamics
item.description: Deep linear networks are simple enough to study analytically but rich enough to exhibit key phenomena of neural network training. Explore their training dynamics interactively.

item.title: On neural scaling and the quanta hypothesis
item.description: one quantum, two quanta, three quanta, four

item.title: Science plays the long game
item.description: Fundamental science as playing the long game

item.title: The scientific method in two steps
Model your model: a path to verifying theories of deep learning
KRR Generalization Tutorial
item.description: Kernel ridge regression provides a clean mathematical setting to study generalization. Explore how the choice of kernel, regularization, and dataset size shape what a model learns.

item.title: Progressive Sharpening Tutorial
item.description: During training, the sharpest direction of the loss landscape grows toward the edge of stability. Explore this phenomenon and its implications for learning rate selection.

item.title: Lazy/Rich Tutorial
item.description: An interactive exploration of the lazy and rich regimes of neural network training — when do networks learn features, and when do they stay close to their initialization?

item.title: The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: Feature learning and the final network weights
item.description: How neural networks learn features and the mathematical structure of final network weights.

item.title: The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: The dynamics of optimization
item.description: Major lines of theoretical research into the optimization dynamics of neural networks, from classical convergence theory to the edge of stability.

item.title: The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: The structure in the data
item.description: How deep networks discover and exploit structural patterns in high-dimensional data.

item.title: The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: Hyperparameter selection (and why theorists should care)
item.description: The theoretical foundations of hyperparameter optimization and its importance for understanding deep learning.

item.title: The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: Places to make a difference
The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: Generalization
item.description: The mathematical theory behind why deep networks generalize well to unseen data.

item.title: The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: Introduction
The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: The average size of hidden representations
item.description: Exploring the mathematical properties of hidden layer representations and their average magnitudes.

item.title: The Learning Mechanic's Quick and Dirty Guidebook: Neuron-level sparsity
item.description: Mathematical analysis of sparsity patterns in neural network activations and weights.
